summary: Phase 3 (Pexels stock-footage, mock-first) tested end-to-end. Appended TestStockFetcher class with 13 new tests to /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py. Full backend suite now 67/67 passing (54 previous + 13 new). Frontend UI flows all verified via Playwright: Find Assets button per scene row, dark modal with MOCK badge, auto-seeded query from scene.search_terms, custom search, media-type tabs (All/Videos/Photos), Attach -> button turns 'ATTACHED', close modal -> inline chip appears in scene row, hover chip -> detach button removes it, Asset Library filter tabs (all/stock/thumbnail_concept) working.

issues: Recharts 'width(-1) and height(-1)' warnings on initial render (carried over from iteration 3; not introduced by Phase 3).

testid_mismatch.issue: Spec/review-request referenced data-testid='stock-tab-all' for the 'All' tab, but the implementation uses 'stock-tab-both' (backing state id 'both'). Not a functional bug — just a naming inconsistency. Either rename the tab id to 'all' or update the docs/testids list to document 'both'.

action_items: OPTIONAL: Normalise StockAssetModal 'All' tab data-testid — currently 'stock-tab-both' but spec says 'stock-tab-all'. Either rename or document.
Pre-existing (iter 3): Recharts width/height warnings on Dashboard — unrelated to Phase 3.
critical_code_review_comments: stock.py: _use_mock() is evaluated on every request — good (lets admins flip USE_MOCK_PEXELS without restart once PEXELS_API_KEY is set). Both conditions (key missing OR flag truthy) correctly force mock.
stock.py: _mock_results() uses SHA1(query) seed + i*7919 offset — deterministic across calls. External_id range 10M-99M avoids collisions with real Pexels IDs (typically 1-20M). Good.
stock.py: picsum.photos is used for mock previews — requires outbound HTTP but that's acceptable in preview; tests correctly don't rely on pixel loading.

routes.py attach_scene_asset: idempotency key (project_id, scene_id, external_id, source) -> 409 is correct. However, the uniqueness is enforced only by an application-level find_one; consider adding a MongoDB unique compound index to prevent a race on concurrent double-attach.

routes.py attach_scene_asset: viewer-role check (raise 403) happens AFTER _ensure_project_access(..., write=True). Since write=True already blocks viewers on owned projects, the explicit check is a belt-and-braces safeguard — fine.

routes.py project_stock_search: falls back to project.topic when body.query is empty; only raises 400 if BOTH are empty. That matches the spec's 'empty scene fallbacks use project topic gracefully'. Note: review-request bullet 'Empty query on /stock-search returns 400' is literally only true when project.topic is also empty — verified with creator projects (all have topic) the endpoint succeeds. Document this or tighten.
routes.py find_scene_assets: priority order body.query > scene.search_terms > visual_direction > project.topic > 'stock' — sensible.
StockAssetModal.jsx: MOCK badge rendered from /api/stock/meta response — UI correctly hides it once USE_MOCK_PEXELS becomes false.

StockAssetModal.jsx: Attach button flips to 'ATTACHED' via local attachedIds Set — does NOT call /api/projects/{pid} GET to refresh, so if the user re-opens the modal fresh the 'already attached' state is lost until next scene re-fetch. Low priority UX polish.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (all Phase 3 UI flows verified: modal open, seeded query, search, tabs, attach, ATTACHED state, close, inline chip, hover-detach, Asset Library filters)

test_credentials: admin@facelessforge.io/admin123, creator@facelessforge.io/creator123. Tests register ephemeral TEST_stock2_* creators for cross-user 403 assertions.
seed_data_creation: TestStockFetcher registers one ephemeral second-creator per run (email TEST_stock2_<uuid>@facelessforge.io) for cross-user 403 tests. Attached stock assets are auto-cleaned up (class-level fixture deletes all assets created during the class). UI test cleaned up its own attached chip via the detach flow.
context_for_next_testing_agent: backend_test.py now has 67 tests (54 existing + TestStockFetcher with 13). TestStockFetcher uses two fixtures: target_project (finds a COMPLETED/SCENES_GENERATED project from the creator and flattens the nested GET /api/projects/{id} response {project, scenes, assets, ...} into a merged dict) and second_creator (registers a throwaway creator). Important: GET /api/projects/{id} returns NESTED { project:{...}, scenes:[...], assets:[...] } — not a flat project — so any new tests that consume that endpoint must read assets from `resp['assets']` (top-level) and project fields from `resp['project']`. Mock determinism tested with SHA1-seeded external_ids. Cross-user 403 tested on find-assets + attach-asset. Duplicate attach -> 409 tested. Video attach preserves duration; image attach has duration=null. Media-type filters strictly enforced. No regressions to auth/forgot-password/projects/generation/exports.
rca of the issue: No production bug found in Phase 3. Only notes: (1) data-testid 'stock-tab-all' mentioned in review-request does not exist — actual testid is 'stock-tab-both' because the state value for the 'All' tab is 'both' (matches backend media_type enum). Main agent should either rename the testid suffix or update the spec.  (2) The initial draft of the backend tests assumed flat project shape and failed with KeyError: 'id'; fixed by flattening the nested {project, scenes, assets} response inside the fixture.
